intro: Cyphochlaena sclerioides, also known as "Tongkat Ali's cousin" (due to its similarity in properties to E. longifolia, a plant commonly known as "Tongkat Ali"), is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include potential health benefits such as improved immune function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced physical performance. Bioactive constituents of Cyphochlaena sclerioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, extracts, and capsules, which are used to support male reproductive health and to alleviate symptoms of fatigue and stress.
